payload.answer: The proposed update appears to be an over-atomized bulk insertion: 500 separate memory cards, one per CSV row. I cannot commit, merge, or validate those cards here. Please choose a repair path.
citations: Input: "LLM proposes 500 memory cards, one per CSV row"
Role contract: "Generate repair/user-choice options only; do not decide, commit, append, merge, overwrite, supersede, or mark anything as saved."
repair_options: Keep the CSV rows as separate candidate items, but review them in smaller batches before any storage action.
Group related rows into fewer candidate memories if the rows describe the same concept or event.
Retain all rows as distinct options only if each row is genuinely independent and non-overlapping.
Reject the bulk atomization and request a normalized summary instead of one card per row.
Ask the user to confirm whether the table should be represented as raw records, separate memories, or a condensed set of facts.
